reason: SIGNED INTEGRAND vs NONNEG HYPOTHESIS: The 1D coarea formula (eq coarea-1d) is stated for g: R -> [0,inf] Borel (nonneg). In Step 2, g(tau) = p^alpha(tau) (d gamma^beta/dt)(tau) phi(gamma(tau)) is NOT necessarily nonneg (p^alpha can have either sign; phi is arbitrary smooth). The proof applies (eq coarea-1d) to a signed function without justification. Fix: either (a) extend the corollary to signed L^1 functions via g = g+ - g- decomposition (standard but must be stated), or (b) note that since f is a diffeomorphism, the result is just the substitution theorem which works for all integrable functions without nonnegativity.
